input:
# Microcycle — Week of 2026-02-23

**Program:** In-Season Basketball Strength & Power Maintenance (2-Day, Low-DOMS)  
**Phase:** Phase 1 — Re-Exposure & Tissue Capacity (Week 1 of 4)  
**User:** Brandon Taylor  

## Schedule
- **Mon (2/23):** Lower Power + Posterior Chain Strength (university performance facility, 2:00–2:45 PM)
- **Tue:** Off / basketball only
- **Wed:** Off / basketball only
- **Thu (2/26):** Total-Body Strength (Upper Emphasis) + Speed/Resilience (university performance facility, 2:00–2:45 PM)
- **Fri:** Off / basketball only
- **Sat:** Off / games/practice as scheduled
- **Sun:** Off / recovery

## Week Overview
Week 1 is a **baseline/output-finding week**: keep reps crisp, stop well short of grindy efforts, and prioritize **low soreness**. Main lifts target **~RPE 7** with conservative loading; if anything feels off from practice load or ankle stability, we reduce ROM/volume first (not intensity). Ankle/foot work is included in small doses both days—**consistent exposure without flare-ups**.

---

# Workout — Monday, February 23, 2026
**Focus:** Lower power + hinge strength + ankle stiffness  
**Location:** University athletic performance facility  
**Duration:** ~45 minutes  

## Warm-Up (8 minutes)
1. **Bike or light jog:** 2:00 easy
2. **Ankles/feet prep (barefoot if possible):**
   - Short-foot holds: **2 × 15 sec/side**
   - Tibialis raises (wall-supported): **1 × 15**
   - Calf raise ISO hold (top position): **1 × 20 sec**
3. **Dynamic mobility (fast, not stretchy):**
   - Leg swings (front/back): **10/side**
   - Lateral lunges (shallow): **6/side**
4. **Hinge + brace primer:**
   - Dowel hip hinge or empty-bar RDL patterning: **2 × 8**
   - Dead bug (slow): **1 × 6/side**

## Main Workout

### 1. Pogo Jumps (in place)
**Target:** 3 × 15–20 sec @ easy–moderate (quiet contacts, stiff ankles)  
- **Set 1:** 15 sec  
- **Set 2:** 15 sec  
- **Set 3:** 15 sec  
**Rest:** 40–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Keep knees mostly straight, bounce from ankles. Stop if ankle feels “wobbly.”

### 2. Box Jump — Stick Landing
**Target:** 4 × 2 @ submax height (RPE 6–7)  
- **All sets:** 24" box × 2 reps (stick 2-sec landing)  
**Rest:** 60–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Land “quiet,” knees track over toes, no rushing the reset.

### 3. Trap Bar Deadlift (high handles if needed)
**Target:** 4 × 3 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 135 lb × 5 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 225 lb × 3 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 275 lb × 2 (warm-up)  
- **Set 4:** 315 lb × 3 (RPE ~6.5)  
- **Set 5:** 325 lb × 3 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 6:** 325 lb × 3 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 7:** 325 lb × 3 (RPE ~7.5)  
**Rest:** 2:30–3:00 between working sets  
**Notes:** Fast concentric, controlled down but not slow. If ankles feel unstable at the start, switch to **high handles** and keep the same RPE.

### 4. Rear-Foot Elevated Split Squat (controlled, no slow eccentrics)
**Target:** 2 × 6/side @ RPE 7  
- **Warm-up:** Bodyweight × 6/side  
- **Set 1:** DBs 40 lb each × 6/side (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 2:** DBs 40 lb each × 6/side (RPE ~7–7.5)  
**Rest:** 60–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Slight forward torso, drive through midfoot. Choose ROM that keeps ankle stable (no collapsing in).

### 5. Standing Soleus Raise (knee bent)
**Target:** 2 × 10–12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 70 lb × 12 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 2:** 70 lb × 12 (RPE ~7.5)  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** 1-sec pause at top; smooth reps. This is “capacity,” not a grinder.

### 6. Pallof Press (cable or band)
**Target:** 2 × 10/side @ controlled tempo  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb × 10/side  
- **Set 2:** 25 lb × 10/side  
**Rest:** 30–45 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Ribs down, don’t rotate—own the exhale.

## Cool Down (4 minutes)
1. **Calf stretch (straight knee):** 30 sec/side  
2. **Soleus stretch (bent knee):** 30 sec/side  
3. **90/90 breathing (feet up on bench):** 2:00 easy nasal breaths  

## Notes
- Keep this session **snappy**—no missed reps, no grinding.
- If you feel any ankle “giving way” on landings, reduce box height and keep the same set/rep scheme.
- Total time target: **~45 min**.

---

# Workout — Thursday, February 26, 2026
**Focus:** Upper strength maintenance + power touch + adductors/ankle  
**Location:** University athletic performance facility  
**Duration:** ~45 minutes  

## Warm-Up (7 minutes)
1. **Row erg or bike:** 2:00 easy
2. **T-spine + shoulders:**
   - Band pull-aparts: **1 × 15**
   - Scap push-ups: **1 × 10**
3. **Ankle/shin quick dose:**
   - Tibialis raises (wall): **1 × 15**
4. **Pressing ramp prep:**
   - Push-ups: **1 × 8** (smooth)
   - Empty bar bench: **1 × 10**

## Main Workout

### 1. Med Ball Scoop Toss (rotational)
**Target:** 4 × 3/side @ crisp intent  
- **All sets:** 10–14 lb med ball × 3/side  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Full hip turn, throw fast, no fatigue chasing.

### 2. Barbell Bench Press
**Target:** 4 × 4 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 95 lb × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 135 lb × 5 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 155 lb × 3 (warm-up)  
- **Set 4:** 175 lb × 4 (RPE ~6.5)  
- **Set 5:** 180 lb × 4 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 6:** 180 lb × 4 (RPE ~7–7.5)  
- **Set 7:** 180 lb × 4 (RPE ~7.5)  
**Rest:** 2:00–2:30 between working sets  
**Notes:** Leave 2–3 reps in the tank. If shoulders feel beat up from contact, use a **slightly closer grip** and pause 1 sec on chest with lighter load.

### 3. Weighted Pull-Up (neutral grip if available)
**Target:** 4 × 4–6 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** Bodyweight × 6 (warm-up, smooth)  
- **Set 2:** +25 lb × 5 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 3:** +25 lb × 5 (RPE ~7.5)  
- **Set 4:** +25 lb × 5 (RPE ~7.5–8)  
- **Set 5:** +25 lb × 4 (RPE ~8)  
**Rest:** 90–120 sec between working sets  
**Notes:** Full hang to chin over bar; stop 1 rep before form degrades.

### 4. Half-Kneeling Landmine Press
**Target:** 2 × 6/side @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 55 lb (bar + plates) × 6/side (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 2:** 55 lb × 6/side (RPE ~7–7.5)  
**Rest:** 60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Squeeze glute of down knee side, ribs down—this is shoulder + trunk control.

### 5. Copenhagen Plank (short lever)
**Target:** 2 × 15–25 sec/side  
- **Set 1:** 20 sec/side  
- **Set 2:** 20 sec/side  
**Rest:** 30–45 sec between sets  
**Notes:** If adductors cramp, shorten lever further and keep time.

### 6. Farmer Carry (heavy, smooth)
**Target:** 2 × 25 m heavy  
- **Set 1:** 80 lb DBs each hand × 25 m  
- **Set 2:** 80 lb DBs each hand × 25 m  
**Rest:** 60–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Tall posture, no side-to-side sway, walk like you’re braced for contact.

## Cool Down (4 minutes)
1. **Doorway pec stretch:** 30 sec/side  
2. **Lat stretch (bench or rig):** 30 sec/side  
3. **Adductor rock-backs (easy):** 1:00 total  
4. **Ankle CARs (controlled circles):** 3 slow circles each direction/side  

## Notes
- Upper work should feel like **practice-ready**, not “gym tired.”
- If pull-ups irritate elbows/shoulders, swap to **heavy lat pulldown**: 180 lb × 6 × 4 sets (match RPE 7–8).
- Total time target: **~45 min**.

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Mon (Lower power/hinge):** Box jump stick work + trap bar DL up to **325×3×3** after ramping; single-leg strength kept tight (**RFESS 40s × 6/side**) to minimize DOMS.
- **Thu (Upper strength/power):** Bench baseline at **180×4×3 working sets** (plus 175×4) and weighted pull-ups **+25×5/5/5/4**—solid in-season intensity without grinding.
- **Constraint monitoring (ankle + soreness):** Plyo volume stayed low and controlled (stick landings, short pogo bouts). If ankle feels unstable next practice day, reduce pogo time to **10–12 sec** and lower box height to **20"** next week.
- **Weak points addressed:** Ankle stiffness (pogo + soleus), trunk anti-rotation (Pallof), adductors (Copenhagen), posture/contact strength (carries).
- **Next week (Phase 1, Week 2):** If readiness is good (no soreness spillover), add **+5–10 lb** to trap bar working sets *or* add **+1 rep total** across the 4 sets while holding RPE ~7; bench add **+2.5–5 lb** if all sets stay ≤RPE 7.

<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Brandon Taylor  
- **Age:** 22  
- **Gender:** Male  
- **Experience level:** **Intermediate** — ~3 years consistent lifting experience; D3 college basketball player, currently **in-season** (as of 2026-02-27)

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):** **Performance (in-season basketball)** — maintain/improve strength and power while prioritizing **explosiveness, durability, and injury prevention**, with training that **minimizes soreness** and does not negatively impact games/practices (as of 2026-02-27)
- **Secondary goals:**
  - Maintain lean mass/strength during season
  - Improve ankle/foot resilience and landing mechanics (as tolerated)
- **Target events/deadlines:** **In-season: ~12 weeks remaining** (as of 2026-02-27)

---

### TRAINING CONTEXT

#### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** **2 days/week**
- **Planned lifting days:** **Mon + Thu**
- **Session duration:** **45 minutes**
- **Time window:** **2:00 PM** (between classes and practice)
- **In-season priority:** Keep fatigue low; avoid high-volume lower-body eccentrics close to games (noted constraint driver)

#### Equipment & Environment
- **Location:** Commercial gym — **university athletic performance facility**
- **Available equipment (confirmed):** barbells, dumbbells, platforms, racks, sleds, plyo boxes (full performance center)

#### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Chronic ankle instability — tapes for games; needs ankle/foot stability emphasis and careful plyo/landing volume (since **2026-02-27**). Monitor tolerance and reinjury risk.
- **[ACTIVE]** Soreness/fatigue management constraint — training must not create excessive DOMS that compromises basketball performance (since **2026-02-27**).

#### Preferences
- **Training style preference (implied by goal):** Athletic performance emphasis (power, strength maintenance, durability), **low-to-moderate volume**, high intent, quality reps
- **Focus areas:** Explosiveness (jump/sprint qualities), posterior chain, single-leg stability, ankle/foot robustness, trunk control/anti-rotation

---

### METRICS
*(No performance metrics provided yet; placeholders created. Update when lifts/testing data are available.)*

**Endurance / Sport (Basketball)**
- In-season status: **Active roster, D3** (as of 2026-02-27)
- Minutes/game, jump testing, sprint times: **Not provided**

**Strength**
- Key lifts (trap bar DL, squat variant, bench, chin/pull-up, Olympic lift derivatives): **Not provided**
- Bodyweight strength (pull-ups, single-leg squat quality): **Not provided**

**Movement Quality**
- Ankle stability: **Chronic instability noted** (as of 2026-02-27)
- Pain status / ROM limitations: **Not provided**

**Body Composition**
- Weight / BF%: **Not provided**

---

### LOG
## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (in-season basketball performance focus)
- Confirmed athlete context: **D3 college basketball**, **in-season with ~12 weeks remaining**
- Set training availability: **2x/week (Mon/Thu), 45 min at 2 PM**
- Equipment confirmed: full university performance center (barbells, DBs, platforms, racks, sleds, plyo boxes)
- Added constraints:
  - **Chronic ankle instability** (taped for games)
  - **Must minimize soreness** to protect on-court performance
- Metrics not yet supplied; awaiting current lift numbers and any jump/sprint/playing-load data

**Missing fields to complete consistency (send when available):** height, bodyweight, position, game schedule density (games/week), current lift numbers (or recent training loads), any current ankle pain level (0–10) and side (L/R), and any recent lower-body injuries (knees/hips/hamstrings).</Profile>

<Plan>## Header
- **Program name:** In-Season Basketball Strength & Power Maintenance (2-Day, Low-DOMS)
- **Program Owner:** GymText
- **User:** Brandon Taylor
- **Goal:** Maintain/improve in-season strength & power while prioritizing explosiveness, durability, ankle/foot resilience, and minimal soreness
- **Duration:** 12 weeks (in-season block) + transition to phase cycling as needed

---

## Program Philosophy
Brandon is an intermediate lifter and in-season D3 basketball athlete with only two 45-minute lifts per week, so the plan emphasizes **high-quality, low-to-moderate volume** training that maintains strength/power without creating soreness that would reduce practice/game performance. We’ll use a **two-day “power + strength” structure**: one day biased toward lower-body force production and posterior chain, and one day biased toward total-body strength with upper emphasis and low eccentric stress. Given Brandon’s **chronic ankle instability**, every session includes targeted **foot/ankle stiffness, tibialis/calf capacity, and landing/force-absorption mechanics** in small, repeatable doses rather than high-volume plyometrics. Progression is primarily **autoregulated (RPE-based)** with small weekly load or rep increases when readiness is good, plus planned **volume deloads** to keep fatigue low across the season.

---

# Phase 1: Re-Exposure & Tissue Capacity (Weeks 1–4)
**Goal:** Establish repeatable outputs, reinforce landing mechanics, build ankle/foot capacity, and set baselines with minimal DOMS.

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Lower Power + Posterior Chain Strength**
- **Focus:** Jump/hinge power, posterior chain strength maintenance, ankle stiffness
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets (not counting brief ankle/activation work)

1) **Pogo Jumps (in place)**: 3 × 15–20 sec @ easy-moderate effort  
   *Builds ankle stiffness/reactivity with low soreness cost.*

2) **Box Jump (stick landing)**: 4 × 2 @ submax height (RPE 6–7)  
   *Landing mechanics + explosiveness without high landing volume.*

3) **Trap Bar Deadlift (high handle if needed)**: 4 × 3 @ RPE 7  
   *Low-skill, high-output hinge for in-season strength with manageable soreness.*

4) **Rear-Foot Elevated Split Squat (controlled, not slow eccentrics)**: 2–3 × 6/side @ RPE 7  
   *Single-leg strength and hip control—use ROM that doesn’t irritate ankle.*

5) **DB RDL**: 2 × 6–8 @ RPE 7  
   *Posterior chain volume without crushing CNS.*

6) **Standing Soleus Raise (knee bent)**: 2–3 × 10–12 @ RPE 7  
   *Soleus capacity supports deceleration/landing and ankle stability.*

7) **Pallof Press (anti-rotation)**: 2 × 10/side @ controlled tempo  
   *Trunk control for cutting/landing.*

---

**Thu — Total-Body Strength (Upper Emphasis) + Speed/Resilience**
- **Focus:** Upper strength maintenance, total-body power touch, adductors/core, ankle/foot
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets

1) **Med Ball Scoop Toss (rotational)**: 4 × 3/side @ crisp intent  
   *Power and sequencing without leg fatigue.*

2) **Bench Press**: 4 × 4 @ RPE 7  
   *Strength maintenance with low DOMS.*

3) **Weighted Pull-Up (or heavy lat pulldown)**: 4 × 4–6 @ RPE 7–8  
   *Upper back strength for contact, posture, and shoulder health.*

4) **Half-Kneeling Landmine Press**: 2–3 × 6/side @ RPE 7  
   *Shoulder-friendly pressing + trunk/hip control.*

5) **Copenhagen Plank (short lever if needed)**: 2 × 15–25 sec/side  
   *Adductor capacity for groin injury risk reduction and lateral mechanics.*

6) **Tibialis Raise (machine or wall-supported)**: 2–3 × 12–15 @ RPE 7  
   *Anterior shin capacity to balance calf work and support decel.*

7) **Farmer Carry**: 2 × 20–30 m heavy @ smooth posture  
   *Grip/trunk stiffness and overall durability with minimal soreness.*

---

### Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
- **Main lifts (Trap Bar DL, Bench, Pull-up):**  
  - Use a **double progression**: stay at the listed sets; add **1 rep total** across sets week-to-week *or* add **2.5–10 lb** when all sets feel like **RPE ≤7**.  
- **Jumps/throws:** keep volume fixed; progress by **quality** (higher jump with same “stick” control) rather than max height.
- **Single-leg + ankle work:** add **1–2 reps** per set before adding load.
- **Deload (Week 4):** reduce working sets by ~40–50% (keep intensity ~RPE 6–7). Maintain jumps/throws but drop to **2–3 total sets**.

Transition to Phase 2 if: outputs feel consistent, ankle tolerates pogo/landing well, and you’re not carrying practice-compromising soreness.

---

# Phase 2: Strength–Power Maintenance / Slight Build (Weeks 5–8)
**Goal:** Maintain (or slightly increase) force production with a bit more intensity while keeping volume tight.

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Lower Power + Hinge Strength (Heavier)**
- **Focus:** High intent + heavier hinge, minimal eccentric overload
- **Volume:** ~12–15 working sets

1) **Pogo Jumps**: 3 × 15–20 sec

2) **Box Jump (stick)**: 5 × 2 @ RPE 7 (still submax)

3) **Trap Bar Deadlift**: 5 × 2 @ RPE 7.5–8  
   *Slight intensity bump; keep bar speed high.*

4) **Step-Up (moderate height)**: 3 × 5/side @ RPE 7  
   *Single-leg force without deep ankle dorsiflexion demands.*

5) **Hip Thrust (barbell or machine)**: 2 × 6 @ RPE 7–8  
   *Glute power/hip extension with low soreness cost for many athletes.*

6) **Soleus Raise**: 2 × 8–10 @ RPE 8

7) **Dead Bug (band resisted if available)**: 2 × 6–8/side @ controlled

---

**Thu — Upper Strength + Total-Body Power**
- **Focus:** Upper strength + power touch; trunk/adductors/ankle
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets

1) **Med Ball Overhead Slam**: 4 × 3

2) **Bench Press**: 5 × 3 @ RPE 7.5–8

3) **Chest-Supported Row**: 4 × 6 @ RPE 7–8  
   *Keeps rowing heavy without extra low-back fatigue.*

4) **DB Incline Press**: 2–3 × 6–8 @ RPE 7

5) **Copenhagen Plank**: 2–3 × 15–25 sec/side

6) **Tibialis Raise**: 2 × 12–15 @ RPE 7–8

7) **Suitcase Carry**: 2 × 20–30 m/side  
   *Anti-lateral flexion for cutting/contact control.*

---

### Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- **Main lifts:** aim to add **2.5–10 lb** every 1–2 weeks while keeping prescribed RPE. If readiness is low (game-heavy week), hold load and focus on bar speed.
- **Power work:** do not chase fatigue—stop sets if jump height/throw speed drops noticeably.
- **Deload (Week 8):** reduce total working sets by ~40–50%; keep intensity ~RPE 6–7; keep movement patterns identical for rhythm.

Transition to Phase 3 if: you’re sustaining intensity well and want to prioritize freshness as the season tightens (typical late-season need).

---

# Phase 3: Peak Freshness / Playoff-Ready Maintenance (Weeks 9–12)
**Goal:** Keep strength/power “turned on” with minimal fatigue; emphasize readiness and ankle durability.

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Neural Primer (Lower)**
- **Focus:** Power + low-volume heavy exposure
- **Volume:** ~8–12 working sets

1) **Pogo Jumps**: 2 × 15–20 sec

2) **Box Jump (stick)**: 4 × 2 @ RPE 6–7

3) **Trap Bar Deadlift**: 4 × 1–2 @ RPE 7–8 (fast singles/doubles)

4) **Split Squat ISO (mid-range hold)**: 2 × 20–30 sec/side @ hard but stable  
   *Tendon/joint-friendly strength with low DOMS.*

5) **Soleus Raise**: 2 × 8–10 @ RPE 8

6) **Pallof Press**: 2 × 8–10/side

---

**Thu — Upper Maintenance + Resilience**
- **Focus:** Maintain pressing/pulling strength; keep shoulders/trunk robust
- **Volume:** ~10–14 working sets

1) **Med Ball Scoop Toss**: 3 × 3/side

2) **Bench Press**: 4 × 2–3 @ RPE 7–8

3) **Weighted Pull-Up (or pulldown)**: 4 × 3–5 @ RPE 7–8

4) **Landmine Press**: 2 × 6/side @ RPE 7

5) **Copenhagen Plank**: 2 × 15–20 sec/side

6) **Tibialis Raise**: 2 × 12–15 @ RPE 7–8

---

### Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- Keep loads **stable** and prioritize bar speed and joint feel.
- If you feel great, you may add **one extra rep** on bench/pull-up sets (within RPE cap), but avoid adding sets.
- **Week 12 taper option (if playoffs / key games):** cut volume another 25–40% (keep a few heavy-but-fast singles/doubles at RPE ~7).

---

## Phase Cycling (After Week 12)
- Default cycle: **Phase 1 (4w) → Phase 2 (4w) → Phase 3 (4w) → repeat** during extended seasons, or transition to an off-season program with higher volume and longer eccentrics.
- **Trigger to move toward fresher phases (Phase 3):** increased game density, travel weeks, noticeable drop in jump/energy, or ankle irritation.
- **Trigger to move back toward Phase 1/2:** schedule lightens, you’re consistently recovering well, and you want slightly more strength/hypertrophy stimulus.

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Program created to match in-season constraints (2×/week, 45 min), emphasize low soreness, include ankle/foot resilience and controlled plyo/landing volume.

--- 

If you share (1) game days each week, (2) ankle side + pain rating (0–10), and (3) approximate working weights for trap bar DL and bench, I can tighten the RPE targets into more precise loading and adjust which day is “lighter” relative to your heaviest on-court demands.</Plan>

**Focus:** Upper strength maintenance + power touch + adductors/ankle  
**Location:** University athletic performance facility  
**Duration:** ~45 minutes  

## Warm-Up (7 minutes)
1. **Row erg or bike:** 2:00 easy
2. **T-spine + shoulders:**
   - Band pull-aparts: **1 × 15**
   - Scap push-ups: **1 × 10**
3. **Ankle/shin quick dose:**
   - Tibialis raises (wall): **1 × 15**
4. **Pressing ramp prep:**
   - Push-ups: **1 × 8** (smooth)
   - Empty bar bench: **1 × 10**

### 1. Med Ball Scoop Toss (rotational)
**Target:** 4 × 3/side @ crisp intent  
- **All sets:** 10–14 lb med ball × 3/side  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Full hip turn, throw fast, no fatigue chasing.

### 2. Barbell Bench Press
**Target:** 4 × 4 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 95 lb × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 135 lb × 5 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 155 lb × 3 (warm-up)  
- **Set 4:** 175 lb × 4 (RPE ~6.5)  
- **Set 5:** 180 lb × 4 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 6:** 180 lb × 4 (RPE ~7–7.5)  
- **Set 7:** 180 lb × 4 (RPE ~7.5)  
**Rest:** 2:00–2:30 between working sets  
**Notes:** Leave 2–3 reps in the tank. If shoulders feel beat up from contact, use a **slightly closer grip** and pause 1 sec on chest with lighter load.

### 3. Weighted Pull-Up (neutral grip if available)
**Target:** 4 × 4–6 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** Bodyweight × 6 (warm-up, smooth)  
- **Set 2:** +25 lb × 5 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 3:** +25 lb × 5 (RPE ~7.5)  
- **Set 4:** +25 lb × 5 (RPE ~7.5–8)  
- **Set 5:** +25 lb × 4 (RPE ~8)  
**Rest:** 90–120 sec between working sets  
**Notes:** Full hang to chin over bar; stop 1 rep before form degrades.

### 4. Half-Kneeling Landmine Press
**Target:** 2 × 6/side @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 55 lb (bar + plates) × 6/side (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 2:** 55 lb × 6/side (RPE ~7–7.5)  
**Rest:** 60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Squeeze glute of down knee side, ribs down—this is shoulder + trunk control.

### 5. Copenhagen Plank (short lever)
**Target:** 2 × 15–25 sec/side  
- **Set 1:** 20 sec/side  
- **Set 2:** 20 sec/side  
**Rest:** 30–45 sec between sets  
**Notes:** If adductors cramp, shorten lever further and keep time.

### 6. Farmer Carry (heavy, smooth)
**Target:** 2 × 25 m heavy  
- **Set 1:** 80 lb DBs each hand × 25 m  
- **Set 2:** 80 lb DBs each hand × 25 m  
**Rest:** 60–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Tall posture, no side-to-side sway, walk like you’re braced for contact.

## Cool Down (4 minutes)
1. **Doorway pec stretch:** 30 sec/side  
2. **Lat stretch (bench or rig):** 30 sec/side  
3. **Adductor rock-backs (easy):** 1:00 total  
4. **Ankle CARs (controlled circles):** 3 slow circles each direction/side  

## Notes
- Upper work should feel like **practice-ready**, not “gym tired.”
- If pull-ups irritate elbows/shoulders, swap to **heavy lat pulldown**: 180 lb × 6 × 4 sets (match RPE 7–8).
- Total time target: **~45 min**.

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Mon (Lower power/hinge):** Box jump stick work + trap bar DL up to **325×3×3** after ramping; single-leg strength kept tight (**RFESS 40s × 6/side**) to minimize DOMS.
- **Thu (Upper strength/power):** Bench baseline at **180×4×3 working sets** (plus 175×4) and weighted pull-ups **+25×5/5/5/4**—solid in-season intensity without grinding.
- **Constraint monitoring (ankle + soreness):** Plyo volume stayed low and controlled (stick landings, short pogo bouts). If ankle feels unstable next practice day, reduce pogo time to **10–12 sec** and lower box height to **20"** next week.
- **Weak points addressed:** Ankle stiffness (pogo + soleus), trunk anti-rotation (Pallof), adductors (Copenhagen), posture/contact strength (carries).
- **Next week (Phase 1, Week 2):** If readiness is good (no soreness spillover), add **+5–10 lb** to trap bar working sets *or* add **+1 rep total** across the 4 sets while holding RPE ~7; bench add **+2.5–5 lb** if all sets stay ≤RPE 7.</Week>

reference.response:
# Week 1 Microcycle Dossier — 2026-02-23 to 2026-03-01
**Program:** In-Season Basketball Strength & Power Maintenance (2-Day, Low-DOMS)  
**Phase:** Phase 1 — Re-Exposure & Tissue Capacity (**Week 1 of 4**)  
**Athlete:** Brandon Taylor (Intermediate, in-season D3 basketball)  
**Sessions:** 2 × ~45 min @ 2:00–2:45 PM (University Performance Facility)

---

## Week Focus / Theme
**Baseline & output-finding week** — crisp reps, **no grinders**, and **minimal soreness** to protect practices/games.  
- **Intensity target:** Main lifts at ~**RPE 7** (leave ~2–3 reps in the tank)  
- **Priority constraint:** **Chronic ankle instability** → low-volume, controlled plyo exposures + consistent ankle/foot capacity work  
- **Adjustment rule:** If practice load/ankle stability is off, reduce **ROM/volume first** (not intensity)

---

## Schedule
- **Mon (2/23):** Lift — Lower Power + Posterior Chain Strength  
- **Tue:** Off / basketball only  
- **Wed:** Off / basketball only  
- **Thu (2/26):** Lift — Total-Body Strength (Upper Emphasis) + Speed/Resilience  
- **Fri:** Off / basketball only  
- **Sat:** Off / games/practice as scheduled  
- **Sun:** Off / recovery

---

## Day 1 — Monday (2026-02-23)
### Lower Power + Hinge Strength + Ankle Stiffness
**Duration:** ~45 min  
**Session intent:** elastic ankle stiffness + clean hinge strength exposure; keep landing volume low and controlled.

#### Key Work (as performed)
| Block | Exercise | Prescription | Load / Result | Notes |
|---|---|---:|---:|---|
| Power | Pogo Jumps | 3 × 15–20s | **15s / 15s / 15s** | Quiet contacts, stop if “wobbly” |
| Power | Box Jump (stick landing) | 4 × 2 @ RPE 6–7 | **24"** × 2 (all sets) | 2-sec stick, reset each rep |
| Strength | Trap Bar Deadlift | 4 × 3 @ RPE ~7 | **315×3, 325×3, 325×3, 325×3** | Final set ~RPE 7.5; fast concentric |
| Single-leg | RFESS | 2 × 6/side @ RPE 7 | **40s×6/side** (both sets) | Controlled; avoid ankle collapse |
| Ankle capacity | Standing Soleus Raise (bent knee) | 2 × 10–12 @ RPE 7 | **70×12, 70×12** | Smooth, 1-sec pause top |
| Trunk | Pallof Press | 2 × 10/side | **25×10/side** (both) | Anti-rotation; controlled breathing |

#### Monday Volume / Metrics (working sets only)
- **Trap Bar DL tonnage:** 315×3 + 325×9 = **3,870 lb**
- **RFESS tonnage:** (80 lb total DB) × (12 reps/leg) × 2 sets = **1,920 lb** (per-leg total: 960 lb)
- **Primary intensity:** RPE ~6.5–7.5 (no grind)

#### Notes / Considerations
- If ankle “gives way” on landings: **drop box to 20"** and keep scheme.
- If ankle feels unstable at setup: use **high handles** and keep RPE target.

---

## Day 2 — Thursday (2026-02-26)
### Upper Strength Maintenance + Power Touch + Adductors/Ankle
**Duration:** ~45 min  
**Session intent:** maintain press/pull strength with low DOMS; add rotational power and resilience work.

#### Key Work (as performed)
| Block | Exercise | Prescription | Load / Result | Notes |
|---|---|---:|---:|---|
| Power | Med Ball Scoop Toss (rotational) | 4 × 3/side | **10–14 lb** × 3/side | Crisp intent, no fatigue chasing |
| Strength | Bench Press | 4 × 4 @ RPE ~7 | **175×4, 180×4, 180×4, 180×4** | Leave 2–3 reps in tank |
| Strength | Weighted Pull-Up | 4 × 4–6 @ RPE 7–8 | **+25×5, +25×5, +25×5, +25×4** | Full hang; stop before form breaks |
| Assistance | Half-Kneeling Landmine Press | 2 × 6/side @ RPE 7 | **55×6/side** (both sets) | Glute squeeze + ribs down |
| Resilience | Copenhagen Plank (short lever) | 2 × 15–25s/side | **20s/side, 20s/side** | Shorten lever if cramping |
| Carry | Farmer Carry | 2 × 25m heavy | **80s DBs × 25m** (2 sets) | Tall posture, no sway |

#### Thursday Volume / Metrics (working sets only)
- **Bench tonnage (working sets):** 175×4 + 180×12 = **2,860 lb**
- **Pull-up external load volume:** +25 lb × (5+5+5+4) = **475 lb** external (BW not included)
- **Primary intensity:** RPE ~6.5–8 (practice-ready)

#### Notes / Considerations
- If shoulders beat up from contact: slightly closer grip + **1-sec pause** with lighter load.
- If pull-ups irritate elbows/shoulders: swap to **Lat Pulldown 4×6 @ RPE 7–8** (example given: 180 lb).

---

## Weekly Progression Snapshot (Key Outputs)
**Strength maintenance baselines established**
- **Trap Bar DL:** top work **325 × 3** (3 sets), ~RPE 7–7.5  
- **Bench Press:** **180 × 4** (3 working sets) + **175 × 4**  
- **Weighted Pull-Up:** **+25 × 5/5/5/4** @ RPE ~7–8  
- **Jump exposure:** Box jump **24" 4×2 stick** + pogo **3×15s** (low landing volume)

---

## Constraints / Monitoring (Ankle + Soreness)
- Plyos were **low-volume and controlled** (stick landings + short pogo bouts).
- If next practice shows ankle instability:
  - Reduce pogo to **10–12 sec** bouts
  - Drop box height to **20"**
- Keep main lift effort submax (no grinding) to avoid soreness spillover.

---

## Next Week Targets (Phase 1 — Week 2)
If readiness is good (no soreness spillover, ankle stable):
- **Trap Bar DL:** add **+5–10 lb** to working sets *or* add **+1 rep total** across the 4 working sets (keep ~RPE 7)
- **Bench:** add **+2.5–5 lb** if all sets stay **≤ RPE 7**  
- Keep jump/throw volume the same; progress via **quality** (speed, crisp landings) rather than height/load.
